title: rubus × substerilis
intro: Rubus × substerilis is a hybrid plant species, resulting from the cross between Rubus calycinoides and Rubus idaeus, commonly known as the black raspberry and the wild raspberry. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The leaves of Rubus × substerilis have been reported to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing oxidative stress, soothing digestive issues, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Rubus × substerilis are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
